skills.name: Instruction Reading
skills.description: Decompose stated requirements into the distinct conditions and criteria that govern a judgment before making it.
skills.boundary: Distinct from applying those criteria to evidence or from judging the underlying content.

skills.name: Attention to Detail
skills.description: Detect small but consequential visual or textual features that determine a judgment.
skills.boundary: Distinct from deciding how strongly the observed evidence supports a conclusion.

skills.name: Calibrated Judgment
skills.description: Match the strength and confidence of a conclusion to the clarity and completeness of the available evidence, and recognize when evidence is too thin to decide at all.
skills.boundary: Distinct from judging whether the evidence is relevant, and from recognizing that the source material itself is unfit to judge.

skills.name: Objectivity
skills.description: Base a conclusion only on the evidence genuinely relevant to it, excluding incidental signals, surface resemblance, and unsupported inference.
skills.boundary: Distinct from confidence calibration when the relevant evidence is incomplete.

skills.name: Difference Detection
skills.description: Identify the specific correspondences and conflicts between two observations, such as matching, added, missing, or differing features.
skills.boundary: Distinct from ranking alternatives or judging their overall quality.

skills.name: Source Validity Judgment
skills.description: Recognize when the source material itself is unfit for the intended judgment, separate from any difficulty in reaching that judgment.
skills.boundary: Distinct from being unable to decide a well-formed case, which is a calibration outcome.

skills.name: Clear Writing
skills.description: Express a conclusion and the specific evidence supporting it concisely, concretely, and unambiguously.
skills.boundary: Distinct from the correctness of the conclusion being explained.
